english: nettle, stinging nettle, gen. zwa-tsód (zwa tsod), being, when young, eaten as greens (v. tsód-ma (tsod ma)); zwa(i)-pyi(mo) (zwa (i) phyi (mo)), 'a-ya-zwa-tsód ('a ya zwa tsod), Wdi., blind or dead nettle; zwa-lcág (zwa lcag) scourge made of stinging nettles, zwa-lcág brgyáb-pa (zwa lcag brgyab pa) to flog with it Cs.; zwa-ber (zwa ber), the smart produced by the stinging of nettles Cs.; zwa-o̦brúm (zwa 'brum) Wdn. (?).
Med., e.g. bad-kan za-k'ur gyur (bad kan za khur gyur) Mng. prob. the same word which Sch. spells za-gu (za gu), explaining it by gonorrhoea, morbid discharge of seminal fluid, semen pruriens.

I. vb., perf. zos (zos), bzas (bzas), fut. bza (bza), imp. zo (zo), zos (zos) (C. *ze* (*ze*)) 1. to eat, both of men and animals, zá-bya (za bya), zá-rgyu (za rgyu) what may or must be eaten, za-číg-pa (za cig pa) Dzl. (perh. better bza-yčíg-pa (bza gcig pa)) one that takes only one meal a day, or perh.: one that takes a solitary meal; zós-pas (zos pas) having eaten Dzl.; zós-pai o̦g-tu (zos pa'i 'og tu) after he had eaten Dzl.; zos-grogs (zos grogs) 'immediately after dinner' (?) Sch.; ma-lús-par zá-ba (ma lus par za ba) Dzl., *dág-mo za-če* (*dag mo za ce*) W., to eat up, consume, to clear the plate, the manger; bzá-ru rún-ba (bza ru rung ba) or mi-rún-ba (mi rung ba) what may or may not be eaten; Dzl. 72, 16 has also a supine zós-su (zos su): bu zos-su o̦ṅ (bu zos su 'ong) she will even be constrained to eat her own young (s.l.c.); žim-du zo (zhim du zo) Zam. may you enjoy your dinner! ni f.; zá-k'ar (za khar) at dinner-time Sch.; za-zá-ba (za za ba) 'to eat often, to be a glutton' Cs. — 2. to live upon, to live by, gla zá-ba (gla za ba) to gain one's subsistence as a day-labourer Dzl. -- 3. to itch, za o̦prúg-pa (za 'phrug pa) v. o̦prúg-pa ('phrug pa). -- 4. fig. for to steal, *kún-ma, gón-mo zos soṅ* (*rkun ma, gong mo zos song*) Ld., a thief, a witch, has made away with it. — 5. fig. of affections of the mind: to entertain, to give way to, kón-kro (khon khro), tsig-pa (tshig pa), té-tsom zá-ba (the tshom za ba) to give way to resentment, anger, doubts. -- II. sbst. food, meat, victuals, za ču žim (za chu zhim) good eating and drinking Mil.; *zá-če zá-če, čó-če* (*za ce za ce, bco ce*) W. to eat food, to prepare food. -- za-rkóṅ (za rkong) v. rkoṅ (rkong). -- za-k'aṅ (za khang) dining-room; eating-house, cook's shop C. -- za-k'u (za khu) v. the preceding article. -- zá-mk'an (za mkhan) one that is eating, an eater. -- *za-čóg* (*za chog*) W. what may be eaten, *za-mi-čóg* (*za mi chog*) what may not be eaten. — *za-túr* (*za thur*) C. chop-sticks. — zá - ma (za ma) food, victuals, zá - ma mi ster ruṅ (za ma mi ster rung) though you do not give me any food Mil. — za-yón (za yon) meat-offering to saints etc. Mil. — For more refer to bza (bza).
1. v. above. — 2. also zá-ma-tog (za ma tog) Ssk. करण्ड, basket, in Tibetan only fig., mostly as a title of books, but also used in connection with mysticism.
trouble, noise Cs., troublesome chatting Sch.; troubled, bewildered, perplexed Schr.; in the passage rmi-lam za-zi (rmi lam za zi) maṅ (mang) Med. it seems to signify troubled dreams.
? *zá-ra pí-mo* (*za ra pi mo*) W. the later part of the afternoon, v. rdzá-ra (rdza ra).
heavy silk cloth, za-o̦g-gi gos (za 'og gi gos) a garment made of it Glr.; za-o̦g dgu brtsegs k'ri (za 'og dgu brtsegs khri) a seat formed of nine silk quilts. — za-báb (za bab) id.

n. of a town or district, acc. to Cs. in Bengal, acc. to Pth. in the north-west of India, by the statements of Lamas the present Mandi, a small principality under British protection, in the Punjâb, between the rivers Byās and Ravi, where there is a sacred lake, celebrated as a place of pilgrimage, from which the Brahmins residing there derive a considerable income.

1. sometimes for yzág-pa (gzag pa), from dzágs-pa ('dzags pa). — 2. sbst., Ssk. आस्रव misery, affliction, sorrow, esp. as a consequence of sin, hence frq. = sin, zág-pa zad (zag pa zad) the woe of this world is over, frq.; zág-pa-med-pai las (zag pa med pa'i las) works spotless or without sin Thgy.; zag-méd-kyi bde-ba (zag med kyi bde ba) untroubled happiness Glr.; zag-bcás (zag bcas) burdened with misery and sin, zag-bcás-kyi las gsum (zag bcas kyi las gsum) the three sinful works Thgy.; zag-bcás-kyi mṅon(-par)-śes(-pa) (zag bcas kyi mngon (-par)-shes(-pa)) Glr. and Thgr.?
1. v. dmár-po (dmar po) extr. — 2. also zaṅ-ziṅ (zang zing), ziṅ-ziṅ (zing zing), yziṅ-ba (gzing ba), v. o̦dziṅs-pa ('dzings pa); W. also: muddled, rather tipsy.
1. sbst. matter, object, goods, = rdzas (rdzas), zaṅ-zíṅ čúṅ-zad tsám-gyi (zang zing cung zad tsam gyi)
